Document:
# Race Comparison Guide
Each playable race in OpenEdge 14 starts with different stat values, reflecting their unique strengths and cultural backgrounds. Some races are min-max designs, excelling in specific stats while sacrificing others.
## Human — The Balanced Choice
[bold]Base Stats:[/bold] - Strength: 5 - Vitality: 5 - Dexterity: 5 - Intelligence: 5
[bold]Racial Ability:[/bold] None — humans gain 3 bonus creation points instead.
[bold]Playstyle:[/bold] Flexible. Humans start neutral and can be built into any role.
---
## Elf — The Mage
[bold]Base Stats:[/bold] - Strength: 4 - Vitality: 5 - Dexterity: 5 - Intelligence: 7
[bold]Racial Abilities:[/bold] - +1 Metamagic (Tier 1) — Reduce mana costs by 10% - +1 Arcane Affinity (Tier 2) — Gain +1 INT modifier
[bold]Playstyle:[/bold] Magic-focused. Elves excel at spellcasting with lower mana costs and better mana pools.
---
## Dwarf — The Warrior
[bold]Base Stats:[/bold] - Strength: 7 - Vitality: 7 - Dexterity: 3 - Intelligence: 3
[bold]Racial Abilities:[/bold] - +1 Alcohol Tolerance (Tier 1) — Alcohol heals 50 HP instead of poisoning - +1 Melee Proficiency (Tier 2) — +0.2x weapon damage multiplier
[bold]Special:[/bold] Can drink certain potions that would poison other races.
[bold]Playstyle:[/bold] Combat-heavy. Dwarves hit hard and survive longer with high physical stats.
---
## Tiefling — The Hybrid
[bold]Base Stats:[/bold] - Strength: 5 - Vitality: 6 - Dexterity: 6 - Intelligence: 5
[bold]Racial Abilities:[/bold] - +1 Infernal Heritage (Tier 1) — Slight fire resistance - +1 Devil's Luck (Tier 2) — Critical hit chance +5%
[bold]Playstyle:[/bold] Balanced combat. Tieflings are tough and skilled at dealing damage.
---
## Silva — The Plant Person
[bold]Base Stats:[/bold] - Strength: 6 - Vitality: 8 - Dexterity: 4 - Intelligence: 4
[bold]Racial Abilities:[/bold] - +1 Regeneration (Tier 1) — Passive health regen +2 HP/s - +1 Root Strike (Tier 2) — Melee attacks apply Rooted status
[bold]Playstyle:[/bold] Tanky. Silva are hard to kill with high health and passive healing.
---
## Comparison Table
| Race | STR | VIT | DEX | INT | Best At | Weakness | |------|-----|-----|-----|-----|---------|----------| | Human | 5 | 5 | 5 | 5 | Flexibility | Nothing special | | Elf | 4 | 5 | 5 | 7 | Magic | Weak physically | | Dwarf | 7 | 7 | 3 | 3 | Combat | Slow, low mana | | Tiefling | 5 | 6 | 6 | 5 | Agile combat | Generalist | | Silva | 6 | 8 | 4 | 4 | Tanking | Clumsy |
---
## Stat Effects
Each stat point affects your character differently:
[bold]Strength[/bold] — Increases melee weapon damage. Base 1.0x at STR 5.
[bold]Vitality[/bold] — Increases maximum health. Each point = +12.5 HP (base 100 HP at VIT 5).
[bold]Dexterity[/bold] — Increases maximum stamina and dodge chance. Base 100 stamina at DEX 5.
[bold]Intelligence[/bold] — Increases maximum mana and spell damage. Base 100 mana at INT 5.
---
## Choosing Your Race
Consider your intended playstyle:
- [bold]Want to cast spells?[/bold] Choose Elf. Their INT 7 and mana-saving abilities make magic feel powerful. - [bold]Want to swing weapons?[/bold] Choose Dwarf. STR 7 + VIT 7 = strong and survivable. - [bold]Want flexibility?[/bold] Choose Human. 3 extra points let you customize however you want. - [bold]Want survivability?[/bold] Choose Silva. VIT 8 is the highest in the game. - [bold]Want balanced combat?[/bold] Choose Tiefling. Good at everything.
Remember: You can also spend creation points to compensate for racial weaknesses. An Elf with +3 to Strength becomes a viable melee mage.
